2026-04-07T02:33:16.8630054Z [2026-04-07T02:30:31Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 11403b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"tools":[{"description":"Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). It also added scenario test coverage (`FleetCRUD.cs`) exercising CRUD operations for ManagedNamespace. The unreleased `1.2.0-beta.2` CHANGELOG section was not updated.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG not updated**: The `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section under `### Features Added` is empty despite ManagedNamespace support being added\\n- **README Examples section is a stub**: The `## Examples` section only links to generic SDK samples; no concrete code examples or snippets exist for any Fleet resource types\\n- **ManagedNamespace not mentioned in README**: The new `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` type, its CRUD operations, and key concepts (DefaultResourceQuota, DefaultNetworkPolicy, etc.) are completely absent from the README\\n- **No Samples test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed samples; the `## Examples` section cannot link to testable code examples\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section to add the ManagedNamespace entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `ManagedNamespace` resource support (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`), enabling creation, update, deletion, and enumeration of managed namespaces within a Fleet.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\n   Provide at minimum two snippet-backed samples:\\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet` – authenticate, create a Fleet resource\\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace` – create a ManagedNamespace under an existing Fleet\\n\\n   Example structure:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n   // Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\n   using System;\\n   using System.Threading.Tasks;\\n   using Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\n   using Azure.Identity;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\n   using NUnit.Framework;\\n\\n   namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples\\n   {\\n       public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n       {\\n           [Test]\\n           [SyncOnly]\\n           [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n           public async Task CreateFleetAndManagedNamespace()\\n           {\\n               #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet\\n               ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n               SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n               ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n               ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\\n               ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n               ArmOperation createOp = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, fleetData);\\n               ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = createOp.Value;\\n               #endregion\\n\\n               #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace\\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection namespaces = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces();\\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n               ArmOperation nsOp = await namespaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, nsData);\\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource ns = nsOp.Value;\\n               Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created namespace: {ns.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n               #endregion\\n           }\\n       }\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Update README** at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`:\\n\\n ManagedNamespace\\n\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` currently has all required sections but needs the following improvements:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action needed |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package | ✅ Present | None |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add bullet points describing Fleet, FleetMember, ManagedNamespace, UpdateRun, Gate resource types |\\n| Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders as described above |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | None |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | None |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | None |\\n\\nFor the **Key concepts** section, add a description along the lines of:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n- **Fleet** (`ContainerServiceFleetResource`): Top-level resource representing a group of AKS clusters managed together.\\n- **FleetMember** (`ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource`): An AKS cluster enrolled in the Fleet.\\n- **ManagedNamespace** (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`): A namespace propagated and managed across all Fleet member clusters, with configurable resource quotas and network policies.\\n- **UpdateRun** (`ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource`): A sequenced rollout of Kubernetes version upgrades across Fleet members.\\n- **Gate** (`ContainerServiceFleetGateResource`): A signal-based gate that can pause an UpdateRun until a condition is satisfied.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 fleet` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 fleet` — if any public types changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have It also added scenario test coverage (`FleetCRUD.cs`) exercising CRUD operations for ManagedNamespace. The unreleased `1.2.0-beta.2` CHANGELOG section was not updated.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG not updated**: The `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section under `### Features Added` is empty despite ManagedNamespace support being added\\n- **README Examples section is a stub**: The `## Examples` section only links to generic SDK samples; no concrete code examples or snippets exist for any Fleet resource types\\n- **ManagedNamespace not mentioned in README**: The new `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` type, its CRUD operations, and key concepts (DefaultResourceQuota, DefaultNetworkPolicy, etc.) are completely absent from the README\\n- **No Samples test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed samples; the `## Examples` section cannot link to testable code examples\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section to add the ManagedNamespace entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `ManagedNamespace` resource support (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`), enabling creation, update, deletion, and enumeration of managed namespaces within a Fleet.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`\\n\\n   Provide at minimum two snippet-backed samples:\\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet` – authenticate, create a Fleet resource\\n   - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace` – create a ManagedNamespace under an existing Fleet\\n\\n   Example structure:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n   // Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\n   using System;\\n   using System.Threading.Tasks;\\n   using Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\n   using Azure.Identity;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models;\\n   using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\n   using NUnit.Framework;\\n\\n   namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples\\n   {\\n       public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase\\n       {\\n           [Test]\\n           [SyncOnly]\\n           [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n           public async Task CreateFleetAndManagedNamespace()\\n           {\\n               #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet\\n               ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n               SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n               ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n               ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets();\\n               ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n               ArmOperation createOp = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, fleetData);\\n               ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = createOp.Value;\\n               #endregion\\n\\n               #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace\\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection namespaces = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces();\\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n               ArmOperation nsOp = await namespaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, nsData);\\n               ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource ns = nsOp.Value;\\n               Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created namespace: {ns.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n               #endregion\\n           }\\n       }\\n   }\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Update README** at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`:\\n\\n ManagedNamespace\\n\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n   ```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` currently has all required Present | None |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | None |\\n\\nFor the **Key concepts** section, add a description along the lines of:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n- **Fleet** (`ContainerServiceFleetResource`): Top-level resource representing a group of AKS clusters managed together.\\n- **FleetMember** (`ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource`): An AKS cluster enrolled in the Fleet.\\n- **ManagedNamespace** (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`): A namespace propagated and managed across all Fleet member clusters, with configurable resource quotas and network policies.\\n- **UpdateRun** (`ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource`): A sequenced rollout of Kubernetes version upgrades across Fleet members.\\n- **Gate** (`ContainerServiceFleetGateResource`): A signal-based gate that can pause an UpdateRun until a condition is satisfied.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build
